COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY DEFENDANT SFS 39, INC.(DBA THE FRANCISCAN CRAB RESTAURANT), A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-24-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT SFS 39, INC. (DBA THE FRANCISCAN CRAB RESTAURANT) MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAYS THIS ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION. THE BROAD ARBITRATION CLAUSES APPLY TO ALL OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS. THE COURT RECOGNIZES THAT THE ISSUE CONCERNING ENFORCEABILITY OF AN AGREEMENT TO WAIVE THE RIGHT TO BRING A REPRESENTATIVE PAGA ACTION IS PENDING BEFORE THE SUPREME COURT AND THAT STATE AND FEDERAL COURTS HAVE DISAGREED ON THE ISSUE. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT, HOWEVER, ALSO GIVES THE ARBITRATOR THE AUTHORITY TO DECIDE ISSUES OF ENFORCEMENT, VALIDITY, SCOPE, AND APPLICABILITY OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT. THEREFORE, IT WILL BE FOR THE ARBITRATOR TO DECIDE WHETHER TO ENFORCE THE REPRESENTATIVE ACTION WAIVER AND WHETHER IT IS APPROPRIATE FOR PLAINTIFFS' REPRESENTATIVE PAGA CLAIMS TO GO FORWARD IN ARBITRATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2013 Continued To Mar-12-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Sfs 39, Inc.(Dba The Franciscan Crab Restaurant), Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-03-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: ALEXEI KUCHINSKY, ESQ., 415.693.9107, FOR PLAINTIFFS.
DAMION ROBINSON, ESQ., 425.241.0379, FOR DEFENDANT SFS 39, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: tHE COURT GRANTS Defendant SFS 39, Inc. (Dba The Franciscan Crab Restaurant) Motion To Compel Arbitration and stays this action pending arbitration. The broad arbitration clauses apply to all of plaintiffs' claims. The Court recognizes that the issue concerning enforceability of an agreement to waive the right to bring a representative PAGA action is pending before the Supreme Court and that state and federal courts have disagreed on the issue. The arbitration agreement, however, also gives the arbitrator the authority to decide issues of enforcement, validity, scope, and applicability of the parties' agreement. Therefore, it will be for the arbitrator to decide whether to enforce the representative action waiver and whether it is appropriate for plaintiffs' representative PAGA claims to go forward in arbitration. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Mtn To Compel Arb
